title: success_criteria machine-checkable
purpose: A1.2 defines success criteria machine checkable for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A1.2` only; siblings under `A1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: At H1 plan approval, the conductor records `success_criteria[]` in `state.goal` as machine-checkable predicates—exit codes, artifact paths, or metric thresholds—that [A1.3](A1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md) and the verifier can run without human judgment.

On each pursuit wake, [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) rejects criteria that cannot map to a script, test, or artifact check; subjective language forces an H2 blocker before any implement step proceeds.
The conductor binds each criterion to a concrete verify command or evidence path in task cards so workers know which checks must pass before `goal.state` may advance past pursuing.
If operators amend success_criteria mid-pursuit, the conductor dual-writes journal and state.json, then re-runs preflight so downstream steps do not pursue an obsolete definition of done.
When verification cannot reconcile criteria against collected evidence, pursuit fails closed at H2 rather than transitioning to achieved or H3 pending.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A1.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A1.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A1.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A1.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A1.2 defines success criteria machine checkable for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system at h1 plan approval, the conductor records `success_criteria[]` in `state.goal` as machine-checkable predicates—exit codes, artifact paths, or metric thresholds—that [a1.3](a1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md) and the verifier can run without human judgment, then on each pursuit wake, [a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) rejects criteria that cannot map to a script, test, or artifact check; subjective language forces an h2 blocker before any implement step proceeds.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent pilot 2026-06-28 -->

A goal that cannot be checked mechanically is not a goal the system can pursue autonomously. This capability requires success_criteria that scripts or tests can evaluate—clear predicates, exit codes, or artifact checks—not subjective “looks good” language.

Success criteria are the bridge between intent (approved at H1) and [goal verification](A1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md). Each criterion should map to a command, file presence, or metric the verifier can run without asking a human. Vague criteria force H2 blockers and stall the always-on loop.

When criteria change mid-pursuit, the conductor must dual-write the update and re-run preflight so downstream steps do not assume an obsolete definition of done. Poor criteria are a common root cause of false “achieved” states; this spec exists to prevent that class of failure.
